top_signals.title: Stocks Rise Ahead of Micron Earnings; US, Iran Offer Conflicting Claims | Bloomberg Brief 6/24/2026
top_signals.summary: US equity futures edge higher ahead of earnings results from Micron, after the Nasdaq 100 closed down by more than 3%. SK Hynix plans to raise $30 billion in a US listing to meet memory chip demand. Oil extends decline amid ongoing US-Iran talks. Max Kettner of HSBC sees an expiry date to US exceptionalism in the near term. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: India pivots to Russian crude and coal to mitigate Iran war fallout - Reuters
top_signals.summary: India pivots to Russian crude and coal to mitigate Iran war fallout  Reuters
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: CryptoQuant warns on Strategy's dividend coverage as cash reserve falls 38%
top_signals.summary: As Strategy's cash reserves are now enough to only pay 14 months of dividends from the previous seven-year level,  CryptoQuant that the company should pause Bitcoin purchases and rebuild the reserve.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: European Stocks Steady Ahead of Micron Earnings  | The Opening Trade 6/24/2026
top_signals.summary:
Equity markets steadied after a brief selloff in top-performing technology names, with Micron’s earnings taking on added significance as a test for the rally in artificial intelligence stocks. Micron’s earnings report on Wednesday afternoon is shaping up to be one of the most important in months as investors find themselves suddenly on edge over the sustainability of the AI rally.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie.
 (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: US gives Cameco-backed Westinghouse $17.5B nuclear boost
top_signals.summary: The goal is to accelerate the deployment of 10 large-scale commercial nuclear reactors by up to three years.
